programme.desc: The Antarctic contains 90% of all ice on earth. This means the islands that surround it play a vital role.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | The Final Countdown
programme.desc: When bad weather strikes, most people head in doors, but we head right into the heart of it. And now you can too, experiencing every thunderbolt, every dust storm, and every flood, all from the comfort of your own home. Let's jump right in!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Slip and Mudslide
programme.desc: Rain. Snow. Lightning. Hurricanes. Dust devils. When these weather phenomena combine, it's usually a recipe for disaster. But on this show, it's just another normal day in the books. It's time for Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Earth, Wind, and Wildfire
programme.desc: We always take weather for granted, but when Mother Nature turns up the heat, she forces us to pay attention. From terrifying twisters to radical rain, we've got it all....and over the next half hour, you'll see 25 of the most insane weather ever caught on camera. Get comfortable, because you're about to experience Extreme Weather Countdown.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Dust Devils in the Wind
programme.desc: Weather is an unrelenting force, tearing through our lives and homes, with no regard for the human condition. But lucky for us, these humans have been conditioned to take out their phones and record whatever is happening, letting us relive the experiences again and again. And when it comes to weather that is out of the ordinary, that's truly where we come alive. Strap in, because you're about to get hit by an Extreme Weather Countdown.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | In Cold Flood
programme.desc: Floods give way to rainbows and tornadoes twist into hailstorms on todays intense Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Timber!
programme.desc: Things are heating up with sweltering heat waves, then cooling back down with icy blizzards on todays Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Who Wants to Play Twister?
programme.desc: Todays forecast: thunder, lightning, and high winds. Oh, and lots of tornadoes.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Lost in the Clouds
programme.desc: Eyelashes freeze, dogs swim in the street, and more of the unexpected on this Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Cold as Ice
programme.desc: Pipes freeze, snow falls, and people slip on the aftermath in todays ice cold Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | The Eye of the Storm
Extreme Weather Countdown | Expect the Unexpected
programme.desc: Youre in for some wild, wild weather today as tumbleweeds, snow, and lightning all pile up!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Slip and Slide
programme.desc: Every weather event you can imagine is making it impossible for people- and cars- to stay on their feet!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Bubble, Bubble, Hail & Trouble
programme.desc: Tornadoes twist at sea, hail pounds the Earth, and trees topple over on todays Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: What in the World?
programme.desc: Perspective-shifting ideas on how to see life on Earth. 1) Can you freeze your body and come back to life? | Shannon N. Tessier 2) How exactly does binary code work? - José Américo N L F Freitas 3) What is Zeno's Dichotomy Paradox? - Colm Kelleher 4) How big is a mole? (Not the animal, the other one.) - Daniel Dulek 5) What on Earth is spin? - Brian Jones 6) If superpowers were real: Invisibility - Joy Lin 7) Is light a particle or a wave? - Colm Kelleher 8) How folding paper can get you to the moon - Adrian Paenza 9) Is there a center of the universe? - Marjee Chmiel and Trevor Owens 10) One is one ... or is it? - Christopher Danielson 11) How simple ideas lead to scientific discoveries - Adam Savage 12) Free falling in outer space - Matt J. Carlson

programme.title: Psych Out
programme.desc: What makes you, well, you? Get to know yourself a bit better with this collection of thought-provoking ideas. 1) How do you know what's true? | Sheila Marie Orfano 2) The link between inequality and anxiety | Richard Wilkinson 3) How climate change affects your mental health | Britt Wray 4) When do kids start to care about other people's opinions? | Sara Valencia Botto 5) Should you trust unanimous decisions? - Derek Abbott 6) Why our screens make us less happy | Adam Alter 7) 3 lessons on decision-making from a poker champion | Liv Boeree

programme.title: Lead the Way
programme.desc: What makes a great leader? Surprising, nuanced approaches on how to inspire and empower others to do their very best. 1) Why great leaders take humor seriously | Jennifer Aaker and Naomi Bagdonas 2) We need leaders who boldly champion inclusion | June Sarpong 3) The breathtaking courage of Harriet Tubman - Janell Hobson 4) Great leadership is a network, not a hierarchy | Gitte Frederiksen 5) How great leaders innovate responsibly | Ken Chenault

programme.title: The Latest in Ideas
programme.desc: New, fresh ideas straight from the brightest minds of our time. 1) My epic journey becoming the fastest person to paddle around Australia | Bonnie Hancock 2) How to choose clothes for longevity, not the landfill | Diarra Bousso 3) How to prevent political corruption | Stephanie Honchell Smith 4) Can we hack photosynthesis to feed the world? | Steve Long 5) Does math have a major flaw? | Jacqueline Doan and Alex Kazachek 6) An election redesign to restore trust in US democracy | Tiana Epps-Johnson

programme.title: The Poetry Corner
programme.desc: Sit back, relax and let the rhythm of poetry ask questions and deliver truths about what it means to be human. 1) Poems of war, peace, women, power | Suheir Hammad 2) A lyrical bridge between past, present and future | David Whyte 3) Metaphorically speaking | James Geary 4) Why should you read Dante's "Divine Comedy"? | Sheila Marie Orfano 5) Can a computer write poetry? | Oscar Schwartz

programme.title: The Poetry Corner
programme.desc: Sit back, relax and let the rhythm of poetry ask questions and deliver truths about what it means to be human. 1) Miles from Any Shoreline | Sarah Kay 2) Insults by Shakespeare - April Gudenrath 3) Suddenly, my body | Eve Ensler 4) The power of unconventional thinking | David McWilliams 5) A meditation on Rumi and the power of poetry | Leili Anvar

programme.title: The Mind Shift
programme.desc: Help your mind stay focused on maintaining and supporting better mental health. 1) How to find humor in life's absurdity | Maira Kalman 2) The benefits of a good night's sleep - Shai Marcu 3) Mental health care that disrupts cycles of violence | Celina de Sola 4) Nature is everywhere -- we just need to learn to see it | Emma Marris 5) Life is hard. Art helps | Liana Finck
